title: T-001 — Planning canonical factory release read-model consumer guards
prompt:
# T-001 — Planning canonical factory release read-model consumer guards

Implement the Planning-side consumer contract for the canonical factory release read model. This is one atomic API/service task for 04-PLANNING-BASIC. Do not create a competing Planning release enum, BOM activation rule, or D365-derived factory availability flag. Discover and import/use the canonical model from 01-NPD T-097 and the Technical adapter/approval contracts T-080/T-081.

Planning must use only canonical release rows with status `approved_for_factory` or `released_to_factory` to admit normal WO creation/release. The WO snapshot must persist the exact `active_bom_header_id`, `active_factory_spec_id`, release event/status metadata, and blocker context used at admission. `pending_npd_release`, `pending_technical_approval`, `blocked`, missing `active_bom_header_id`, or missing `active_factory_spec_id` must block normal WO creation/release and must surface typed `release_blockers` to UI callers. D365 SO pull may create draft demand candidates only; D365 Built/export/preload/sync status is optional metadata and never source-of-truth. Rework WOs remain explicit `is_rework=true` exceptions and must not become a backdoor for unreleased FG/WIP.

Scope includes manual WO create, D365 SO draft review/release, cascade preview/confirm, material/BOM snapshot creation, and Planning WO list/detail badge data. For cascades, every FG/WIP/intermediate node must resolve through the canonical read model before confirmation, and the created WO DAG must snapshot the active BOM/spec IDs used per node.

RED first: write failing tests for approved/released success, pending Technical approval block, blocked release with typed blockers, missing active IDs block, D365 Built/export no-op, override limited to approved/released canonical rows, and cascade node block rollback. Then implement service/action code. Close with tests and evidence.

pipeline_inputs.description: Planning consumer guards and WO snapshot contract for canonical factory release read model.
pipeline_inputs.details:
One task type: API/service/action tests. UI visual implementation is not included except returning blocker/badge data for existing Planning screens. The canonical model is owned by 01-NPD T-097; Technical T-080/T-081 performs approval/adaptation. If canonical implementation is not present, stop with a blocker rather than inventing a fallback enum.

Additional acceptance detail preserved during validation hardening:
- Given cascade confirmation includes a pending/blocked node, the whole DAG creation rolls back and blockers identify the offending product/node.
- Given a user attempts BOM override, only canonical approved/released rows can be selected and audit records old/new active BOM/spec IDs.
acceptance_criteria: Given canonical status is approved_for_factory or released_to_factory and active_bom_header_id plus active_factory_spec_id exist, normal WO creation/release snapshots those IDs and succeeds.
Given canonical status is pending_npd_release, pending_technical_approval or blocked, Planning blocks normal WO creation/release and returns typed release_blockers without mutating WO state.
Given D365 SO import has Built/export/preload/synced metadata but canonical status is not factory-usable, only draft demand is created and release remains blocked.

Given the task is closed out, when reviewers inspect evidence, then these additional requirements are proven together: Given cascade confirmation includes a pending/blocked node, the whole DAG creation rolls back and blockers identify the offending product/node.; Given a user attempts BOM override, only canonical approved/released rows can be selected and audit records old/new active BOM/spec IDs.
test_strategy: RED unit tests for release guard status matrix and missing active IDs.
RED action/service tests for manual WO create, D365 draft review, and cascade rollback.
risk_red_lines: Do not create a Planning-owned release enum/model.
Do not infer factory availability from D365 Built/export/sync state.
Do not select latest active BOM/spec outside canonical active_bom_header_id and active_factory_spec_id.
Do not let rework bypass manufacture unreleased FG/WIP.

cross_module_dependencies.reason: Canonical factory release status/read model owner.
Technical adapter exposes factory-use guards and badge/blocker mapping.
Scanner consumes released WO/material reservation contracts and produces execution events.
Consumes Technical factory-use adapter/status/blocker contract; Planning is not source-of-truth for BOM/spec approval.
Consumes canonical NPD release read model for factory usability.
